results.possibleIdentity: John B. Gibson is an electrical engineer and technical consultant who was active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering (BSEE) from the West Virginia Institute of Technology (now West Virginia University Institute of Technology). His technical expertise focused on analog computers, cybernetics, and electrical engineering systems. In the late 1960s, he was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that matched artists with engineers for collaborative projects; in this capacity, he was listed as a technical resource in fields such as cybernetics. Professionally, Gibson held roles with several major corporations, including the American Electric Power (AEP) Corporation—which has significant operations in Huntington, West Virginia—as well as Bechtel Power Corporation, United Engineers, IBM, and Compaq. He later transitioned into executive leadership, serving as the President of United Intelligence Corporation and as the twice-elected Chairman of the Orange County Section of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE).
